philosophy: Extreme diversification through factor-neutral, autonomously managed sub-pods. Each PM runs an isolated book targeting idiosyncratic alpha. Center risk management prevents correlated blowups via non-negotiable drawdown enforcement. Ken Griffin's core thesis: diversified alpha is more scalable and less fragile than concentrated bets.
gross_net_style: +/- 2% Strict Net Bounds — violations trigger immediate forced rebalancing

factor_exposure.value_vs_growth_label: Hard Neutralized — factor exposure models run continuously; no style drift allowed
factor_exposure.beta: Zero — hard neutralized at portfolio level
factor_exposure.momentum: Neutralized — factor exposure models run continuously
factor_exposure.idiosyncratic: Very High — all alpha must be stock-specific

sector_concentration: Hard cap at 5% per sector — enforced daily by risk systems

risk_constraints.forced_liquidation_trigger: PM Drawdown > 3% triggers 50% gross cut; > 5% triggers full pod termination

preferred_instruments: Sector ETF Hedges (XLF, XLK, XLE, XLV)

historical_regime_behavior.q4_2018_rate_hike: Center risk cut PM books aggressively as factor correlations converged to 1.0 near December lows — systematically sold quality at the bottom.
historical_regime_behavior.march_2020_covid: Survived via immediate gross deleveraging (200% to 80% in 5 sessions). Missed V-shape recovery entirely.
historical_regime_behavior.q3_2022_inflation: Forced sector neutralization via XLF shorts as rates sector outperformed uncontrollably.

macro_reaction_function: Drawdown survival mechanics override all fundamental judgment. Macro shocks force correlation convergence → center risk cuts PM allocations mechanically → reinforces downward momentum in liquid names.
confidence_notes: Very High Confidence. Pod center risk architecture is inflexible by design. Liquidation triggers are mathematical and fully automated.
